BC Health CareConnect Repository Map — V35 accuracy + line endpoint fix

Edit is OFF by default. Turn Edit ON to move icons, canvases, line anchors, labels, and colours.Autosaved

Selection

Shift-click items, or Shift-drag a box around items, then drag any selected item to move the group. The previous Simple/Full toggle was removed because it was altering visibility state in a confusing way.

Create / layout

New items appear in the middle of your current view. Drag icons or spaces anywhere. Resize spaces with the blue handle.

Line tools

No line start selected yet. Select an icon first.

Canvases / focus

These jump around the same large canvas so you do not lose/recreate work.

V23 repository coverage

Visible now: expanded source-system boxes for HLTH, PHSA/CareConnect, VCH/PHC shared cluster, FHA, IHA, NHA, VIHA, document categories, API consumers, data wishlist, and data locks.

Still needs source validation: exact push/pull direction, owner boundaries, launch dates, and final interface names for some yellow/unknown nodes.

Timeline overlay

Small overlay only. Full history lives in the separate evolution map.

Organizations, hide items only

These hide icons and related arrows. The larger infrastructure boxes stay visible.

Connection types

Layers

Document categories

These highlight/hide the specific document nodes: Clinical Care Plan, Clinical Summary, Notes & Assessments, MHSU, Pharmacist Consult Notes, Community Paramedicine, Family Health Forms, Goals of Care, and dictation/transcription.

Edit selected

Click an icon, arrow, or infrastructure box first. Drag icons and boxes to move them. Use the blue handle to resize boxes. Box titles, subtitles, icon labels, icon subtitles, and line labels are editable: click the text and type, or double-click a line label.

Save / Export

Legend

Light blue = internal data movement
Orange = auto data push HL7 v2
Purple = auto data push CDX
Red = auto data push FHIR
Green = manual API push/pull request
Black dashed = Data Arch / API pull requests
Thick black = special architecture dependency / reference

Yellow databases mean “need to clarify / unsure,” not necessarily data locked. Data Locks are kept as their own separate section.

Source references

Reference images are no longer large canvas boxes. Use these buttons when you need them.
